arts.ttl: The Stoke City midfield Mark Robins must pick to stay up
arts.sum: We have assembled a 10-games-to-go fans' panel to run through key questions ahead of this fixtured to talk positives or negatives, midfield balance, most important players and whether Stoke will stay up. Mark Calladine: Stokes best midfield for me is Ben Pearson, Wouter Burger and Jordan Thompson.

arts.ttl: EFL answer to parachute payment problem haunting Stoke City
arts.sum: The Premier League has been opposed to changes to parachute payments, which are made to relegated clubs in declining amounts over their first three seasons back in the Championship. EFL chairman Parry wants 25 per cent from massive Premier League TV deals to be filtered down, at the same time as setting up a merit-based payment system with a 2:1 ratio between top and bottom in the top division and repeated in the Championship.

arts.ttl: Blackburn Rovers signing's Nottingham Forest fuel in play-off pursuit
arts.sum: The Blackburn Rovers left-back has experienced plenty in his first six weeks at Ewood Park

EFL chairman Rick Parry has been pointing to the huge financial chasm between the top flight and the rest – and then between clubs in receipt of parachute payments and the rest beyond that – which encourages some owners to spend a lot more money than they bring in to try to win promotion to the big time. If you look at the amount of money that Championship clubs routinely spend on average on wages, it’s in excess of 100 per cent of turnover.

arts.ttl: Wouter Burger opens up Stoke City transfer decision, Mark Robins and why he won't sulk
arts.sum: His response to Stoke City rejecting a transfer bid from FC Midtjylland at the end of the January window has been his best run of performances of the season, and possible his whole time at the club. He said: “I was a little bit disappointed for a few hours but I move on, I want to be there for the team and that’s also why I played earlier in the season through my injury – because we have a really nice team and I have a lot of joy to drive every morning to Stoke.

arts.ttl: Wouter Burger's big lesson at Stoke City - 'I should have chosen for myself'
arts.sum: The 24-year-old admits he was not playing well as he tried to manage an ankle problem which was affecting his movement and decision making, and it took taking a step back for a couple of weeks at the turn of the year to finally get it right. Wouter Burger says he has learned he is doing himself and the team a disservice by trying to play through an injury, like he did for much of the first half of this season.
